items.title: Trade deal in hand, EU carmakers look to India | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Trade deal in hand, EU carmakers look to India
Teaser: The ink is still wet on the EU-India trade deal, but carmakers in Europe are already welcoming an agreement that will give them greater access to the world’s most populous country. But what is India for a market? How does it compare to the US and China? And is the deal really a good one for car imports? DW speaks with Jonathan O’Riordan at the European Automobile Manufacturers’ Association.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Has the probability of US military action against Iran increased? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump is urging Iran to reach a nuclear deal or risk a new U.S. attack. This threat follows the deployment of the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier and other warships to the Middle East. 
In response to Washington's announcements, Iran's UN Mission in New York issued a statement on X.

Chapters:

items.title: How deadly is it compared to the Coronavirus? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Health officials in India have confirmed cases of the deadly Nipah virus, prompting authorities across Southeast Asia to boost security measures and prevent further spread. The World Health Organization lists Nipah as a priority disease due to its potential for rapid outbreaks. There is no approved vaccine or cure. Singapore, Hong Kong, Thailand, and Malaysia have taken urgent steps to prevent the virus from spreading beyond India, implementing airport temperature checks and other screening measures.

Chapters:
0:00 Nipah virus triggers Asian airports screening measures
2:11 Kaja Abbas, Associate Professor of Infectious Disease Epidemiology

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Activists say pressure from abroad is essential for anti-government protesters inside Iran | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Iran-born rapper Meraj Tehrani witnessed the deadly protests in his home country. With the toll ranging from thousands to tens of thousands of people killed in a crackdown by security forces, Tehrani is calling on foreign countries to help Iranians overthrow the regime and regain their freedom. DW's Birgit Maass interviewed him in London, where he lives. 

Instagram: @merajofficial

items.title: What prompted Ruth Gruenthal to take this step at the age of 103? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Once unthinkable: At 103, Holocaust survivor Ruth Gruenthal has reclaimed the German citizenship the Nazi regime stripped from her because she was Jewish.
After surviving Nazi persecution and rebuilding her life in the U.S., Ruth became a psychotherapist and raised a family spanning four generations — many of whom have now also reclaimed German citizenship.
Born in Hamburg in 1922, imprisoned in France, and forced to flee Nazi persecution as a teenager, Ruth rebuilt her life in the United States, where she lived for decades. But recent political developments, rising antisemitism, and fears of growing authoritarianism in the U.S. have shaken her sense of security.
Ruth is not alone. An increasing number of Jewish Americans with family histories shaped by the Holocaust are applying to restore German citizenship — not necessarily to leave, but to have a safeguard: the option to move to Germany should conditions in the United States deteriorate further.
Germany allows victims of Nazi persecution and their descendants to reclaim citizenship that was deliberately taken from them. In New York alone, applications have more than doubled in recent years.

#holocaustsurvivor #germancitizenship #usa #trump 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: US embassy removes flags honoring Danish soldiers in Afghanistan | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The US embassy in Copenhagen removed 44 flags honoring Danish soldiers killed in Afghanistan. The flags had been placed after President Donald Trump criticized allies' military contributions. Hundreds of new flags were later put up in response.

items.title: Who needs whom the most at the moment? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Russian President Vladimir Putin welcomed Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa to Moscow for talks. This is the second time Putin has hosted al-Sharaa since rebels toppled Syria's longtime dictator, Bashar al-Assad, over a year ago. Russia supported the Assad regime during the conflict and is still hosting the former leader in exile. 

Chapters:

2:33 Anna Borshchevskaya, The Washington Institute Senior Fellow

items.title: Is the way to the next stage of the Gaza peace plan paved after funeral of last hostage? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israel has held a funeral for the last remaining hostage recovered from Gaza. 24-year-old police officer Ran Gvili was killed while fighting Hamas militants during the October 7 terror attack in 2023. That attack killed 1,200 Israelis and ignited a war that has claimed the lives of over 70,000 Palestinians. Today's funeral marks the end of a painful chapter for Israelis and paves the way for the next phase of President Trump's peace plan. 

0:00 Will the funeral of the last Israeli hostage pave the way to new steps towards peace?

3:26 Nomi Bar-Yaacov, a peace negotiator with the Geneva Centre for Security Policy says the challenges ahead are 'absolutely vast'

#israel #gaza #israelhamaswar

items.title: Minneapolis protesters cheer as Border Patrol commander Greg Bovino is removed from post | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Protesters in Minneapolis celebrated after immigration chief Greg Bovino was removed from his role as “commander-at-large” of the US Border Patrol. Bovino is said to be returning to his former post in California.  His departure comes amid intense backlash after two U.S. citizens were fatally shot by federal immigration agents in Minneapolis, sparking widespread protests and political pressure.

items.title: Pyongyang says it is preparing to unveil the next stage of its nuclear strategy | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>North Korea is testing its next generation of nuclear weapons. The US may be threatening to attack Iran over its nuclear program.  But, for the time being, that's not the case for North Korea.  In fact, Pyongyang says it is preparing to unveil the next stage of its nuclear strategy.  
This follows the test of what the North calls a new upgraded launcher, which would be capable of carrying out nuclear strikes. This latest plan is set to be revealed at a rare ruling party congress — the first in five years — due to be held in the coming weeks.  
What could be in this "next stage" plan, and what does this latest signal from Pyongyang mean for security in the region and beyond?

0:00 North Korea is testing its next generation of nuclear weapons
2:32 William Alberque, Former NATO official, Senior Fellow with the Pacific Forum

#northkorea #nuclearweapons #kimjongun

items.title: Asian countries tighten airport screenings after India reports Nipah virus cases | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>There were no reported cases of the Nipah virus outside India, but several Asian countries introduced sweeping health precautions after India confirmed two cases in West Bengal. Indian authorities say they have contained the outbreak.
Thailand, Indonesia, and Vietnam increased airport screenings, reinforced border checks, and urged stricter food safety measures to prevent the virus from spreading.

items.title: ICE agent attempts to enter Ecuador's consulate in Minneapolis | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Local media shared footage showing an ICE agent attempting to enter Ecuador’s consulate in Minneapolis, but consulate officials prevented the entry and protected Ecuadorian citizens inside. Ecuador's government said it filed a formal protest with the US Embassy in Quito over the incident.

items.title: US President Trump tells Iran to make a deal or face attacks | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump has warned Iran that 'time is running out' over nuclear talks, saying that the next attack will be 'far worse'.
 
It follows the arrival of the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier and other warships to the region. The convoy was deployed from the South China Sea to the Arabian Sea. Fighter jets are positioned in the region as well.
 
Trump also announced that another US military fleet is on its way to Iran.  
 
"There's another beautiful armada floating beautifully toward Iran right now. So we'll see. I hope they make a deal. I hope they make a deal."

00:00 Trump: "Next attack on Iran will be far worse" 

00:45 Meir Javedanfar, Iranian-Israeli lecturer and author, Reichman University, Herzliya

05:15 Bradley Bowman, Center on Military and Political Power at the Foundation for Defense of 
Democracies, Washington, D.C.

11:37 Michael O'Hanlon, Senior Fellow for Foreign Policy, Brookings Institution

#iran #trump #usa
